title: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
description: Hunter S. Thompson's 1971 "savage journey to the heart of the American Dream" — a hallucinatory account of a road trip to Las Vegas fuelled by an alarming quantity of controlled substances. Written in the "Gonzo" style Thompson pioneered, it was challenged in US schools and libraries for its glorification of drug use and its savage satire of American culture.
description_book: Maverick author Hunter S. Thompson introduced the world to "gonzo journalism" with this cult classic that shot back up the best seller lists after Thompson's suicide in 2005. No book ever written has more perfectly captured the spirit of the 1960s counterculture. In Las Vegas to cover a motorcycle race, Raoul Duke (Thompson) and his attorney Dr. Gonzo (inspired by a friend of Thompson) are quickly diverted to search for the American dream. Their quest is fueled by nearly every drug imaginable and quickly becomes a surreal experience that blurs the line between reality and fantasy. But there is more to this hilarious tale than reckless behavior, for underneath the hallucinogenic facade is a stinging criticism of American greed and consumerism.
description_ban: Removed from some U.S. libraries due to drug use, profanity, and countercultural themes.
inclusion_rationale: Banned in US libraries in 1985 due to depictions of drug use and offensive language, reflecting censorship of countercultural literature.
title_native: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas

The novel explores themes of trust, loyalty, and passion against the backdrop of political intrigue and suspense.

title: Intense Pleasure
description_book: Her name is Summer, as beautiful and occasionally fierce as her name suggests. Her past has just come back to haunt her, and suddenly she s not sure who she can trust. Her longtime confidantes Falcone and Raeg seem to have Summer s best interests at heart. . .but it s clear that their desire for her still burns between them. Can they find a way to keep Summer safe or will a bitter rivalry stand in their way? Falcone and Raeg need to work together, now more than ever, if they intend to protect Summer from an old enemy who knows her dangerous secret. When Summer s identity as a sweet, Southern socialite gets out, the two men must find a way to draw out the ruthless assassin on her trail. . .and keep their illicit romance with her under wraps. But some passions are so intense they can t remain hidden. What are Falcone and Raeg willing to risk for the woman they both crave like no other?

inclusion_rationale: Banned in Malaysia in 2017 under the Printing Presses and Publications Act for sexual content.

title: The Prophet
description_book: The Prophet is a book of 26 poetic essays written in English in 1923 by the Lebanese-American artist, philosopher and writer Khalil Gibran. In the book, the prophet Almustafa who has lived in the foreign city of Orphalese for 12 years is about to board a ship which will carry him home. He is stopped by a group of people, with whom he discusses many issues of life and the human condition. The book is divided into chapters dealing with love, marriage, children, giving, eating and drinking, work, joy and sorrow, houses, clothes, buying and selling, crime and punishment, laws, freedom, reason and passion, pain, self-knowledge, teaching, friendship, talking, time, good and evil, prayer, pleasure, beauty, religion, and death.
description_ban: In 1992, "The Prophet" by Kahlil Gibran was banned in Malaysia by the Ministry of Home Affairs under the Printing Presses and Publications Act, with the official reasons cited as religious blasphemy and immorality. The ban was gazetted on August 20, 1992, and the book was published by William Heinemann Ltd. No notable public statements or legal responses from the author or publisher regarding the ban have been documented.
inclusion_rationale: Banned in Malaysia in 1992 for religious and moral reasons under the Printing Presses and Publications Act, indicating significant suppression of philosophical and spiritual discourse.

title: The Joke
description_ban: In 1968, "The Joke" by Milan Kundera was banned at the national level in Czechoslovakia following the Warsaw Pact invasion, which ended the Prague Spring. The official reason for the ban was its political content, particularly its critique of the communist regime. The ban remained in effect until 1989, when it was lifted as part of the broader political changes in the country.

title: Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung
description_book: "Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung," commonly known as the Little Red Book, is a collection of sayings and writings by Mao Zedong, presenting his communist ideology and political thought. The text encompasses themes of revolution, class struggle, and the role of the individual in society, serving as a guide for the Cultural Revolution in China. Its significance lies in its influence on Maoist movements worldwide and its role as a symbol of political ideology and propaganda during the 20th century.
description_ban: In 1967, "Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung" was banned in Malaysia by the Ministry of Home Affairs under the Printing Presses and Publications Act, with the official reason cited as its political content. The ban was gazetted on May 25, 1967 (P.U.231).
censorship_context: In 1967, Malaysia's Ministry of Home Affairs banned "Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung" under the Printing Presses and Publications Act, citing its political content as the reason for the ban.
inclusion_rationale: Banned in Malaysia in 1967 for political content under the Printing Presses and Publications Act, reflecting government suppression of dissenting political thought.
